segments.caption: The video segment functions as a closing credit sequence, intercutting close-up shots of various individuals with on-screen text listing production roles and acknowledgments. The sequence begins with a smiling man with graying hair in a dark patterned shirt, followed by a close-up of a robotic head with a wide, open-mouthed expression and blonde hair. The credits then overlay the faces of different men, including one with short dark hair and a blue shirt, another with wavy hair in a dark shirt, and a man with a beard and mustache, all shown in close-up with neutral or smiling expressions. The text includes credits for the film by Dorothee Kaden, camera operators, sound team, video editors, graphics, imaging, sound mixer, unit manager, production manager, and commissioning editors from HR and ARTE. The sequence also acknowledges Prof. Hans-Jochen Menzel and students at Berlin’s Ernst Busch Academy of Dramatic Arts, and notes the production by HR in collaboration with ARTE, with the English version by Deutsche Welle 2022. The robotic head reappears multiple times, sometimes with a furry hat labeled 'Furhat Robotics,' and in one frame, it is shown with a wide, toothy grin. The final frames show a bald man in a light gray shirt smiling, with the full production credits displayed over his face. The lighting is warm and consistent, with a focus on facial expressions and the text overlays, creating a professional and reflective tone for the closing credits.
